[2018-03-15 01:26] elyot: @upskuttle All questions are welcome ^_^
[2018-03-15 01:26] elyot: The problem is that this just isn't any kinda effort we really have any time to lend any support to
[2018-03-15 01:27] elyot: we mess with our own APIs all the time
[2018-03-15 01:27] elyot: there's no documentation that we can really share without just sharing our source code
[2018-03-15 01:28] elyot: we've had a few people interested in doing this kind of thing before and it always turns out to be much much much more work than they expected, it's not really a reasonable thing for one person to do
[2018-03-15 01:28] .jrkirby (jrkirby): When do we get a frog based unit?
[2018-03-15 01:29] upskuttle: my prev experience with community clients is for forged alliance forever, in which case the game’s company went defunct & dev support was in the form of “one dude violated his nda and kept some communication protocols on his computer” — honestly, when i asked cel about this & she said “maybe they could tell you about their api” this came as a genuine shock for me :p
[2018-03-15 01:30] elyot: you can open up the .swf file and look at it
[2018-03-15 01:31] elyot: like we have a secure layer and a non-secure layer, and stuff wrapped in amf format, and there's a whole transport/courier protocol layer that handles message ordering and disconnect/reconnect and nodeswitching
[2018-03-15 01:31] elyot: that's before you get to a single actual api call
[2018-03-15 01:33] upskuttle: i understand the scale of the project (hence the doubt that i would be able to complete a full implementation entirely by myself, & the desire to instead work on the groundwork on which some eventual solution might be laid) & i totally get it if any kind of openness is impossible, for time or security reasons
[2018-03-15 01:34] elyot: feel free to poke around but uh... you've got your work cut out for you...
[2018-03-15 01:35] elyot: (but I don't have any personal objections)
[2018-03-15 01:35] elyot: (just please don't DDOS us and be responsible if you notice any problems!)

[2018-03-17 08:36] coscott: So I am surprised and confused about your decisions about dealing damage to golden armor units after killing all other units. The smallest K-complexity thing to do seems like it is to make it so that you can never damage golden armor units. The problem with this of course that it makes it so that a player can stall by buying one engineer a turn and waste a lot of time after they lost. It seems like the damage for golden armor units was added to avoid this problem, which seems reasonable, as it is a nice quality of life adjustment for a small K-complexity penalty. However, it occurs to me that you could get the same benefit out of just saying that if all of your units have golden armor, you lose the game. This seems simpler than the current rules, having less stalling than the current rules, and will almost never come up, and when it does come it will almost never change who wins. It also seems more intuitive, since golden armor represents that the units are not there yet, they appear at the start of your turn. If you only have golden armor units, you have no units. So why did you go the way you did with damaging golden armor?

So why did you go the way you did with damaging golden armor?

[2018-03-17 08:49] coscott: actually, I guess it is not THAT weird that someone might hit you with Tia, and just barely kill your last drone, and under the existing rules, you can buy a drone at the end to save yourself and pull off a win, so I wouldn't say it never changes who wins, but this seems pretty rare, and it seems intuitive to me that the tia player should win there, since the whole "under construction" and "blocks immediately" to me seems like a more complicated way of saying that you get the unit at the start of your next turn vs at the end of this turn, and if you get the unit at the start of your next turn, you are out of units.

[2018-03-19 15:45] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): @Plato @Elyot @mrguy888 
The idea is simply to protect new players from themselves. If you hop right into ranked, you permanently change your ELO. Since new players tend to overestimate their skill, it is important to protect them for a while so that they can not blow up and leave the game after a few ranked losses. Casual matchmaking might be poor, but even if you have a casual mmr used for matchmaking, the nature of the game is that it will draw a lot of good players from other games that have turned RNG heavy, so new players will have VERY different entry levels. Balancing matchmaking for casual will be very hard until that gets solved
[2018-03-19 15:48] zugz: "permanently change elo"?
[2018-03-19 15:48] zugz: once i get better my elo will get better with me, presumably

[2018-03-19 16:15] .bky_1556 (bky): @Elyot  Personally, I found events much more frustrating than any other mode.
[2018-03-19 16:23] elyot: @Plato People improve by 500 Elo within the first few hours of playing. There is a very low supply of players who haven't made that 500 Elo improvement yet, so it's impossible to find good opponents for them. It's the same as the bronze league matchmaking problem in StarCraft 2, and they have a MUCH LARGER supply of players than we do
[2018-03-19 16:24] elyot: it's not that we don't have enough players, it's that players stay at that level of ability for such a short amount of time that they don't have a long enough opportunity to be opponents for other players at the same level
